rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARTHA MELGAR ROMERO
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/13/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) Notice was not given to Joseph Edward Romero or to Angelina Romero. (2) The copy of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance attached to the notice of hearing was barely legible. Please provide a courtesy copy of the proof of service to the assigned examiner by 11/4/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (As this successor executor herself stated in Attachment 3g(1)(d) to her petition filed on 11/28/23, a successor executor was needed in order to sign the correcting deed to fund this decedent's trust with the estate's assets and then record an affidavit death of joint tenant. It appears this has been done now, as the 850 petition was granted on 7/25/24, so this estate should be able to close. Ideally, a petition to terminate for lack of assets will be filed before the 11/13/24 hearing, in which case, this petition can be taken off calendar.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID FRANK PHILLIPS II
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/13/24 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice has been filed. Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) No bank statements have been filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement to verify the costs of sale. (5) A petition for final distribution must also state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (6) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (7) The accounting does not appear to begin with the correct total of the assets on hand as of 12/31/21. (A review of the register of actions shows that this is the SECCOND account, although the caption of this pleading did not clearly indicate this.) (8) Receipts for any prior distributions must be filed. (9) It appears that far more than the FDIC insured amount is being held at one bank. (10) Reimbursements to any individual must indicate the original payee(s). Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/1/24. (As no courtesy copies were provided, the petition and accounting have not been fully reviewed yet.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FRANCISO ESTRABAO'S LIVING TRUST DATED AUGUST 25, 2004
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/22/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/05/2024): 1) The petitioner resides in Sacramento County. Please provide specific examples of the day-to-day activity of the trust carried on by the petitioner as trustee within San Francisco County. See Probate Code sections 17002 & 17005(a)(1). 2) Has the petitioner served a trustee notification under section 16061.7? If so, please provide a copy. 3) Is the subject real property currently occupied by any person (e.g., tenant)? If so, the name of each person having possession of the property must be provided by a verified allegation. Each occupant must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. 4) The prayer for relief includes the validity of the trust and confirmation of the petitioner as successor trustee, but the petition caption does not. CRC 7.102. 5) The address of each beneficiary listed in paragraph 18 of the petition must be provided by a verified allegation. See section 17201 & CRC 7.902. 6) The legal description of the property must include the APN (Lot and Block numbers). 7) The proposed order must be revised to state the correct property address and legal description. 8) Please provide a copy of a deed or other recorded instrument or a title report showing how the settlor held title at the time of his death. 9) Please provide the name and address of the personal representative of the settlor's estate, together with a copy of letters issued to the representative. If no probate administration is pending or anticipated: i) Did the settlor die testate? If so, please provide a copy of his will. The name and address of each devisee must be provided by a verified allegation, and notice must be given. Any devisee who is a not a beneficiary of the trust must be served in the manner required by sections 851(b)(3) and 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. ii) If the settlor died intestate, are the beneficiaries listed in paragraph 18 the only children the settlor ever had? Note: An appearance may be required on 10/22/2024 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, unless papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are on file.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF REISEL BERGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to determine whether the proposed guardian ad litem should or should not be appointed to represent the minor's interest in this estate before ruling on this petition, in light of the following: 1) The decedent's will left everything to the petitioner spouse, except for $60,000 cash gifts to the decedent's siblings. 2) The minor does not qualify as an omitted child to take a statutory share under Probate Code section 21620, as the decedent created the will 7 months AFTER the child was born. 3) No facts have been alleged as potential grounds to contest the will. 4) Even if the will is invalid, the petitioner will receive all community property by intestate succession, and the minor will only receive 50% of the decedent's assets that still must be established as his separate property. 5) The $60,000 cash gifts to the decedent's siblings will be the only assets subject to this probate, as the petitioner, according to his supplement, has no intent to make a section 13502 election to probate the decedent's assets passing to him or the petitioner's share of community property already belonging to him. 6) If the minor petitions for a family allowance under section 6540, the allowance can only be paid out of the $60,000 probate estate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WAI FOON MOY LEONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of closing the estate will be set for 9/30/25. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 9/30/25 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARLA JEAN CAMPODONICO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/22/2024. The following issues to be cleared two weeks prior to the hearing: (1) Petition to seek appointment of Special Administrator pursuant to Local Rule 14.17 to access the decedent's safe deposit box so that the decedent's original will can be located. Once the original will is located, the petition shall be amended accordingly. (2) If the original will cannot be located from the safe deposit box, Petition does make any allegation relating to Prob. Code sec. 6124. It looks like the original will is locatable. (3) Petition #8 to identify the trust beneficiaries as the petitioner/proposed executor is the trustee of the sole devisee trust. said beneficiaries must be noticed if applicable. (4) Petition #8 needs to set forth all heirs at law, including both side of material/paternal next of kins so that all known heirs can be properly noticed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LARRY DENNIS MENKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/29/24. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel on 9/9/24.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY J. LACER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
